# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an ARM template for a complete Azure web application stack (App Service, SQL Database, Storage Account). Focus on core resources and connectivity. We'll add diagnostic settings and monitoring in the next iteration after we verify the basic setup works.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

# Sample azuredeploy.json for ARM template to create a complete Azure Web App stack (App Service, SQL Database, Storage Account) with connectivity core resources 
parameters:
- name: appNamePrefix
  type: string
  
resources:
- type: Microsoft.Web/sites@2018-02-01
  name: ${appNamePrefix}webAppServicePlan
  apiVersion: "2018-02-01"
  location: eastus
  kind: AppServicePlan
  
- type: Microsoft.Web/sites@2018-02-01
  name: ${appNamePrefix}webApp
  apiVersion: "2018-02-01"
  location: eastus
  kind: Site
  properties: {}   # Add your app settings here. For example, 'scmType': 'LocalGit' or other values like this for the source control feature of Azure DevOps integration with GitHub/Bitbucket etc., which is not mentioned in requirements but can be added as per requirement if needed
  dependsOn: ["${appNamePrefix}webAppPlan"] # Dependency on App Service Plan to ensure before creating app.  
   
- type: Microsoft.Sql/servers@2018-06-01
  name: ${appNamePrefix}sqlServer
  apiVersion: "2018-06-01"
  location: eastus
  kind: Server
    
- type: Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts@2019-06-01   # Azure Storage Account for Blob storage, File share etc., can be used as per requirement if needed   
  name: ${appNamePrefix}sa
  apiVersion: "2019-06-01"
  location: eastus
  kind: StorageAccounts    
  
# Adding Diagnostic Settings and Monitor for monitoring the app service. This is not included in requirements but can be added as per requirement if needed, like below example of creating a Log stream diagnostic setting which logs to Application Insights resource with name ${appNamePrefix}AppInsights 
- type: Microsoft.Insights/components@2018-05-01   # Azure Monitor Component for collecting and analyzing app service metrics etc., can be used as per requirement if needed   
  name: "${appNamePrefix}monitor"
